findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier priority system for notifications requires changes to the notification rendering logic and user interface components. This dependency on the UI and backend systems must be addressed to ensure proper functionality.
assumptions: The current notification system can be modified to support priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Assess current UI components for compatibility with priority levels.

findings.title: Push Notification Delivery Failure on Android 14
findings.description: The failure of push notifications on Android 14 devices poses a critical risk to user engagement. This issue needs immediate resolution to prevent loss of users and trust.
assumptions: The workaround of re-enabling notification permissions is not a sustainable solution.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate the root cause of the delivery failure and implement a fix.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: The implementation of ML-based notification batching requires a robust ML model and integration with user engagement data. This is a complex task that should be planned for V2 after addressing immediate issues.
assumptions: The existing data infrastructure can support ML model training.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Plan for data collection and model training in V2.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This dependency on the frontend and backend systems must be managed to ensure timely delivery.
assumptions: The existing backend can support new API endpoints.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Develop the API and UI components in parallel to meet MVP timelines.

findings.title: Notification Delivery Latency Issue
findings.description: The current notification delivery latency exceeds the SLO, which can lead to user dissatisfaction. This needs to be addressed to maintain service quality.
assumptions: The proposed fix of moving to async writes will resolve the latency issue.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement the proposed changes to the delivery pipeline as soon as possible.

summary: The analysis highlights critical dependencies and risks associated with the SmartNotify project. Key components such as notification priority levels and delivery latency need immediate attention, while ML-based batching and user preferences can be planned for future phases. Addressing the Android 14 delivery issue is paramount to maintaining user trust.
